name: CROUZON SYNDROME
definition: CRANIOFACIAL SYNOSTOSIS / DYSOSTOSIS Apert syndrome without syndactyly skull + cranial base deformities characterized by craniosynostosis, maxillary hypoplasia,
distribution: cloverleaf appearance (coronal view) + bilateral frontal indentations (axial view) of skull
discriminating_features: hypoplastic maxilla → relative prominence of mandible
calcification of stylohyoid ligament (in 50% of patients > 4 years of age)

general: hypoplastic maxilla → relative prominence of mandible
calcification of stylohyoid ligament (in 50% of patients > 4 years of age)
musculoskeletal deformities (7%) but no limb anomaly
cloverleaf appearance (coronal view) + bilateral frontal indentations (axial view) of skull
